slice.name: Linear full-lifecycle scope reconciliation
slice.purpose: Reduce the remaining tracker-scope ambiguity before Judge/PM closeout by uploading an owner-visible scope note attachment to Linear JSC-363 and recording repo-local evidence that Linear is planning and ownership truth, not implementation proof.
feedbackSignals: The active goal explicitly says this is not Phase 1-only.
The plan frontmatter still requires Linear action before full lifecycle delivery claims.
Live Linear JSC-363 title and description still describe a Phase 1 issue while the repo goal board now governs PU-000 through PU-016 plus adopted audit and SPG gaps.
Prior linked-issue failures showed external checkers need explicit relationship classification.
slice.rootOperationalFailure: Tracker wording and PR attachment history can make full-lifecycle progress look either narrower than the actual goal or more complete than the evidence supports.

externalMutation.reason: The available Linear connector exposes attachment creation but not issue title/description update. A tracked Markdown attachment uploaded as base64 content is the narrowest available owner-visible mutation that records the scope note without overwriting issue fields.
requiredEvidence: sha256 digest of the exact Markdown bytes uploaded
redacted create_attachment argument summary including issue, filename, contentType, title, and subtitle
returned Linear attachment id or URL
post-mutation get_issue evidence showing the attachment is visible
explicit verdict that issue title/description fields remain stale unless a later Linear issue-field update occurs

nonGoals: Do not claim runtime producer emission, delivery-truth consumption, merge readiness, or PR stack merge completion.
Do not rewrite the Linear issue title or description unless an update tool is available and separately reviewed.
Do not mutate code modules in this slice.

validationPlan.expected: Confirms current Phase 1 wording and attachment history before mutation.
Uploads the exact scope note bytes as a Linear attachment; receipt records digest, filename, title/subtitle, returned attachment id or URL, and any connector failure.
Confirms the new attachment is visible after mutation. If it is not visible, local state must record blocked_external_tracker_mutation and must not mark tracker scope mitigated.
Goal board remains valid after recording the slice receipt.
Latest route receipt remains current or ancestor-classified with explicit head relation.
Digest matches the digest recorded in the receipt and uploaded attachment evidence.

acceptanceCriteria: Linear JSC-363 has a visible uploaded attachment explaining that the repo-owned goal is full lifecycle while the original issue wording remains Phase 1.
Repo-local notes record the exact distinction between tracker scope, implementation evidence, PR state, review state, CI, merge readiness, and Judge/PM readiness.
The receipt and state update do not mark the parent goal complete.
The slice records whether Linear issue-field update remains blocked by available connector capability.

The slice verdict is no stronger than tracker_scope_note_attached_fields_stale unless issue title and description are actually updated or an owner-visible acknowledgment explicitly accepts attachment-only mitigation.
Future scope-note attachments supersede earlier scope-note attachments only when the newer attachment digest, Linear attachment id or URL, and post-fetch evidence are recorded in the latest receipt.
